description: Stack buffer overflow in SpiderMonkey's WebAssembly GC fromCharCodeArray path. CVE-2025-13016 (sec-high, MFSA 2025-87, Firefox 145). StableWasmArrayObjectElements::StableWasmArrayObjectElements() in js/src/wasm/WasmGcObject.h used std::copy(array->inlineStorage(), array->inlineStorage() + array->numElements_ * sizeof(T), ownElements_->begin()). The source iterator is uint8_t* (byte-typed) while the destination iterator is T* (e.g., uint16_t* for i16 arrays). std::copy deduces the element count from the source iterator range: numElements * sizeof(T) iterations of destination-sized writes, meaning for i16 arrays the copy writes numElements * sizeof(T) = 2N elements of uint16_t (4N bytes) into an ownElements_ inline stack buffer sized for N elements (2N bytes) — classic stack-buffer-overflow. The fix (commit 75ef3f79aae344c1d0398787cab666a6a73176c7 by Yury Delendik, 2025-10-15, r=rhunt; Phabricator D267284) introduces a typed inlineArrayElements<T>() accessor and restricts the copy to numElements elements of the correct type. Triggered by wasm:js-string builtin fromCharCodeArray on an array-of-i16 under oomTest. Reporter: Igor Morgenstern (Aisle).

fixes.description: Bug 1992130 - Fix arguments types for std::copy. r=rhunt
